ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for fish-and-egg salad with customisable toppings; FOODON_00002809 (salad food product) used as closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (Salads) is the closest defensible category; no specific code exists for fish-and-egg salad with variable toppings and no sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this composite salad dish.

main_ingredients: assorted vegetable toppings (e.g. cucumber, tomato, corn, carrot)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g, representing a full plate of fish salad with egg and three vegetable toppings as typically served at a Singapore Western-style hawker stall or cafe.

nutrient_source.title: Fish Salad with Egg (3 Toppings), No Sauce — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish (cr_id F08010025). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, potassium, and several vitamins are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care, American Diabetes Association (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. GI is estimated at 32 (low) based on the analogous food 'mixed salad with protein' from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), which reports GI values of 28–38 for protein-dominant mixed salads with minimal starchy carbohydrate. Carbohydrate content is 1.87 g per 100 g; at a 300 g serving size, available carbohydrate is approximately 5.6 g, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 1.8 — negligible. The GI estimate is conservative and clinically consistent with the very low carbohydrate content of this dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 291 mg per 100 g; no added sauce eliminates a major sodium source
hypertension.guidance: At 291 mg sodium per 100 g, this dish is well below the 400 mg threshold for hypertension concern; patients should request minimal added salt during fish preparation and may use lemon juice or herbs as sodium-free flavour enhancers.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (1.87 g per 100 g) and low sugar (1.7 g per 100 g) with high protein (5.69 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The negligible carbohydrate load and high protein from fish and egg make this dish one of the most glycaemically stable salad options available; patients should ensure fish is grilled rather than battered and monitor egg intake if managing cholesterol alongside diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (2.99 g per 100 g), no sauce, high protein, and vegetable toppings support a heart-healthy profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low total fat, absence of sauce, and lean protein from fish and egg make this dish suitable for cardiovascular disease management; choosing oily fish such as salmon or mackerel will further increase omega-3 intake, and patients should avoid battered or fried fish preparations.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No sauce, low saturated fat, anti-inflammatory potential from oily fish and vegetable toppings
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is well-suited for rheumatoid arthritis management due to its absence of sauce, low saturated fat, and potential for omega-3 benefit when oily fish is chosen; nightshade-sensitive patients should avoid tomato, pepper, or eggplant toppings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Salad with Egg (3 Toppings), No Sauce is a freshly assembled Western-style salad dish commonly available at Singapore hawker centres and Western-style cafes. It consists of a portion of cooked fish — typically grilled, poached, or baked — served over a bed of fresh mixed greens, accompanied by a hard-boiled or soft-boiled egg, and three customer-selected vegetable toppings such as cucumber, cherry tomato, corn, shredded carrot, or beetroot. The sauce is deliberately omitted, making this one of the lowest-sodium variants in the fish salad series. The dish has a light, clean flavour profile with a tender fish texture, creamy egg, and crisp fresh vegetables. Nutritionally, it is low in calories (57 kcal per 100 g), very low in carbohydrates (1.87 g per 100 g), and provides a meaningful protein contribution (5.69 g per 100 g) from both fish and egg. Sodium is moderate at 291 mg per 100 g, significantly reduced by the absence of dressing or sauce. Dietary fibre (1.66 g per 100 g) comes from the mixed greens and vegetable toppings. This dish is clinically relevant for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and weight, owing to its low glycaemic load, lean protein, and minimal processing. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free, and is halal-suitable when fish and egg are prepared without non-halal additives.

confidence_reason: All core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010025), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The input record carries a confidence of 0.95 and review_status of reviewed. The match is exact. GI is estimated from a peer-reviewed analogous source (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) and is clearly documented as an analogous estimate.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish (F08010025) as of the accessed date. GI is estimated at 32 (low) based on analogous protein-dominant mixed salad data from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care, peer-reviewed); glycemic load per 300 g serving is approximately 1.8, which is negligible. This dish is sold in Singapore in a Western-style hawker or cafe context; local-language names in Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi have been provided as translations to satisfy G6 multilingual compliance for Singapore-served dishes. The dish is classified as halal-suitable assuming standard halal fish and egg preparation; consumers should verify with the specific vendor. Calcium (33.0 mg per 100 g) and iron (0.84 mg per 100 g) are reported by HPB and included. The three toppings are customer-selectable and variable; nutrient values reflect the HPB composite average for this dish category.
